event: Noli Me Tangere — The Risen Christ appears to Mary Magdalene
symbols: Christ's outstretched hand — the gesture of gentle refusal, indicating His transition to a glorified state
Mary Magdalene kneeling — devoted love and recognition of the risen Lord
dark garden setting — the garden tomb at dawn, echoing Eden's garden where death entered and now life returns
blue drapery on Christ — heavenly nature of the resurrected body
description: A dark, atmospheric painting showing two figures in a nocturnal garden setting. On the left, a semi-nude male figure draped in blue cloth stands near a tree trunk, extending his right hand in a gesture of gentle prohibition. On the right, a woman in golden-brown robes kneels and reaches toward him with an expression of astonishment and devotion. The dark background with faint moonlight and distant hills creates a somber, intimate atmosphere.
theological_description: This painting depicts the profoundly intimate encounter described in John 20:14-17, when the risen Jesus first appeared to Mary Magdalene outside the empty tomb. When she recognized Him and reached out, He said 'Do not hold on to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father.' This mysterious command reveals a crucial theological truth: the Resurrection inaugurates a new mode of Christ's presence. The physical intimacy of the earthly ministry must give way to the spiritual communion of the ascended Lord who will be present through the Holy Spirit. Mary Magdalene, the first witness to the Resurrection, becomes the 'apostle to the apostles' — sent by Christ Himself to announce the foundational truth of Christianity. The garden setting creates a deliberate theological echo of Eden: in a garden humanity fell, and in a garden the New Adam rises to restore all things. Christ's gesture is not rejection but redirection — pointing Mary and all believers toward the coming gift of the Spirit, through whom Christ would be present always, to the end of the age.

scripture_refs.reason: The exact moment depicted: Mary Magdalene recognizes the risen Jesus who tells her not to hold on to Him
Mary goes to the disciples announcing 'I have seen the Lord,' becoming the first resurrection witness
Mark confirms Jesus appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom He had cast out seven demons
The garden setting echoes Eden where God walked in the garden — now the risen Christ walks in the garden of the new creation
Christ as the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep — the theological significance of what Mary witnesses
